ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was created */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text of the tweet */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet received */,
  "link" TEXT /* Link to the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies the tweet received */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of quote tweets the tweet received */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "retweet_of_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "geo_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the tweet */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter for iPhone, Twitter Web App) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user is following */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the user is verified */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "description" TEXT /* User's bio or description */,
  "website" TEXT /* User's website URL */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "conversations" (
  "conversation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conversation */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "parent_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent tweet (if applicable) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the conversation was created */,
  "is_direct_reply" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the tweet is a direct reply */,
  "conversation_thread_id" INTEGER /* ID of the conversation thread */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conversation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conversations_parent_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conversations_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtag" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag text */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that used the hashtag */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the hashtag was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtag_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mentions" (
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mention */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that mentioned the user */,
  "mentioned_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who was mentioned */,
  "mention_type" TEXT /* Type of mention (e.g., user, list, hashtag) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mention_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mentions_mentioned_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("mentioned_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mentions_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each link */,
  "link" TEXT /* Link URL */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that shared the link */,
  "domain" TEXT /* Domain of the link */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the linked content */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the linked content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_links_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., likes, retweets, replies) */,
  "engagement_count" INTEGER /* Count of the engagement type */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of impressions */,
  "engagement_rate" TEXT /* Engagement rate (calculated as a percentage) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the analytics entry was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role-permission assignment */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)
